title: Treat BrandBuild and AI Video as the same product family
decision: Allow BrandBuild and AI Video Studio naming to coexist as one product family rather than treating them as separate repos or products.
reason: User explicitly said AI Video and BrandBuild are the same.
impact: Repo cleanup and roadmap decisions focus on removing unrelated product work instead of renaming everything immediately.

title: Remove unrelated legacy runtime source from BrandBuild
decision: Keep the canonical BrandBuild repo focused on BrandBuild surfaces and block reintroduction of unrelated legacy runtime features.
reason: The repo had already experienced cross-product contamination and needed stronger boundaries.
alternatives_considered: Allow mixed product runtime features to coexist in the repo
Delay cleanup until after future feature work
impact: BrandBuild stays cleaner, and future work can build on a more trustworthy surface.

title: Use .project-memory as the initial durable memory location
decision: Store the first repo-backed project memory set inside .project-memory at repo root.
reason: It was the fastest way to initialize durable memory inside the repo during cleanup work.
alternatives_considered: Only human-facing markdown files at project-memory
impact: Project memory existed immediately, but duplicate memory locations later needed consolidation.

title: Send BrandBuild cleanup upstream on main
decision: Push the BrandBuild cleanup and memory initialization upstream on main rather than leaving it local-only.
reason: User explicitly asked to send all changed repos.
alternatives_considered: Delay the push until all naming drift was resolved
impact: The canonical repo became safer to build on without waiting for every later cleanup.

title: Expose system readiness inside the product
decision: Add an in-product settings and control-center surface that shows integration readiness, architecture gaps, and next actions.
reason: The product needed a visible operator-facing truth layer instead of leaving audit state buried in code and chat.
alternatives_considered: Keep readiness knowledge only in docs
Wait until all integrations were finished before exposing status
impact: BrandBuild now has a clearer internal operating surface at /dashboard/settings.

title: Canonicalize structured memory in docs/project-memory
decision: Use docs/project-memory as the canonical structured memory layer, mirror it into .project-memory to avoid stale loads, and keep project-memory as the human-facing operating summary requested by the user.
reason: The repo had diverged across three memory locations and needed a single structured source of truth without destroying history.
impact: Future sessions can reconstruct state reliably while still preserving the earlier memory location.

title: Use shot-grouped compare outputs as the reviews surface
decision: Keep /dashboard/reviews as the compare route and rebuild it around shot-grouped candidates with media previews, provider context, and focused review details rather than adding a separate compare route.
reason: The product needed a first-class comparison workflow quickly, and the existing reviews surface already owned selection logic and review context.
alternatives_considered: Keep the existing flat review list and defer compare UX
Create a brand new /dashboard/compare route before improving the reviews workspace
impact: BrandBuild now expresses its multi-model value proposition much more clearly without changing the route structure or schema.
